rulings.Case Title: MARIA COREAS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 30, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration sustained. The court does not rule on disputed facts. Plaintiff's objections to Valdez declaration sustained, and objection to Khuu Exhibit "A" sustained, all other objections overruled. As to the first and third causes of action for sexual harassmsent/hostile working environment, Chan's behavior although rude, inappropriate, and offensive, was not severe and pervasive as defined by case law. (See Mokler v. County of Orange (2007) 157 Cal.App. 4th 121, Defendants UMF #3.) No triable issue of fact created that as soon as the MTA became aware of Chen's inappropriate sexual conduct, it took immediate remedial action. (Defendants UMF 3-4.) It is undisputed that plaintiff did not complain of Chen's behavior until December 2006 and the City proceeded to change Chen's shift and eventually terminated him as soon as they verified the nature of his behavior. As to the second cause of action, for Discrimination, no triable issue of fact created that plaintiff suffered an adverse employment action. As to the fourth cause of action for retailiation, no triable issue of fact raised: plaintiff suffered no adverse employment action and there is no evidence that her conditions of employment were materally affected. As to the sixth cause of action for Failure to prevent, there are no triable issues of fact regarding harassment and retailiation. The fifth cause of action for Intentional Infliction of emotional distress is conceded. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ASIF AIVAZOV et al VS. JOYCE COX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Rehearing Of Special Motion To Strike; Declarations Of Denis Stroud And Stas Portyansky
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 30, 2009, line 17, PLAINTIFF AIVAZOV, ASIF MINOR, PORTYANSKY, SABINA, STAS PORTYANSKY Motion For Rehearing Of Special Motion To Strike. Motion for reconsideration pursuant to CCP section 1008 is denied. Insufficient showing of new facts, circumstances or law to justify reconsideration. Motion for new trial pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 657 is denied. CCP section 657 is applicable to this dispositive motion. (See Cassady vs. Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 220.) However, upon further review of the authorities cited and the evidence presented, the court finds no persuasive grounds to grant the requested relief. Defendants made a threshold showing that the complaint arises from defendants' protected activity: calling the police. (See Chabak vs. Monroy (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 1502, and Hagberg vs. California Federal Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350.) Plaintiffs fail to establish a probability that they will prevail on their claims. Insufficient evidence of a valid oral lease. No showing that defendants' actions based on discrimination. Objection to late filed opposition overruled, plaintiffs responded on the merits. Defendants'additional objections sustained as to Portyansky declaration objection #1 only, remaining objections overruled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY E. BARRERA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL COR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 30, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO,AMENDED DEMURRER. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. No facts alleged that defendant owed a duty. Government Code section 815 states that direct tort liability of public entities must be based on a specific statute declaring them to be liable, or at least creating some specific duty of care. See Eastburn vs. Regional Fire Protection Authority (2003) 31 Cal. 4th 1175, and Munoz vs. City of Union City (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1077. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VINOD KRIPALANI et al VS. PAUL MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 30, 2009, line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT VINOD KRIPALANI, ASYA KRIPALANI MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. The complaint only contains conclusory allegations but no supporting facts for the punitive damages claim. Cross-complainant must plead specific facts (events, dates, etc.) supporting malice, oppression, fraud, or despicable conduct. =(302/CWW)
